Okay, so we're going to go ahead and add the MX records to the domain that we've chosen. So we go into our domain, noticing that it is locked down and it has SSL enabled. We're going to edit the zone records. To add the MX records, the initial one you're going to add is the text that verifies the domain and I've already done that part. But what you would do for that one is you would go in here to txt, put the at symbol and put your value there and that will give you and then obviously save it and then that will give you your verification. And then to add the MX records, you're going to choose MX. You don't want to edit anything above because those are your existing records. So come down to the bottom, choose MX. It's going to give you these values here and so you're going to go and put the at symbol here, paste your value, take the period off, click save and it closes out every single time. So we'll wait for it. Let's see, go back into edit zone records because we have to put our second one in, MX record, get our next value. We're going to actually edit that one because I left the period at the end. All the way to the bottom, MX records at paste, save. And this is the last one. So after this is done, then the email should work just fine. Here we go and that's how we put our zone records in. That's it. It'll tell you if you have it set up right. Another good way to test it is to see if your email sends or if you can receive an email. So let me send a test email. And it'll take a minute for it to update. But yeah, it works just fine. So that's how you put your MX records in.
